title: Feature: use ZIP for oci-archive
body: I think it would be beneficial if skopeo could work with ZIPs in addition to tars. Besides the general flexibility, I would also hope that accessing oci contents could be made to work without unpacking the entire archive, but accessing members directly as necessary. Especially when loading a bunch of images into a registry, this would speed things up a bit.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

At a higher level is that a static archive is _extremely rarely_ the most efficient way to represent/copy images; focusing on efficiency of an inherently inefficient format is not the best use of resources. (For moving a large number of images, I generally recommend using a short-term registry server backed by a volume, copying images to that registry; moving the volume’s contents to the destination site; and setting up another short-term registry and copying from that registry to the final destination. Alternatively, using a higher-level mirroring tool like `oc-mirror`.)

Specifically, a single-file archive of _any_ kind can’t be efficiently updated to replace an image; does not reasonably allow concurrent writes; and the `oci` format can’t bit-for-bit-identically represent non-OCI images (nor, currently, signatures). All of those things are possible with a registry.

As for ZIP in particular, if the input is a seekable uncompressed tar, we _could_ read it directly from files without first extracting everything. (That isn’t implemented for `oci-archive:`, but it exists in `docker-archive:`.) So, I don’t think we need to introduce a _new_ file format, and complicate the interoperability landscape, for this purpose in any case.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
